intent: Keep autodoro from firing its focus-block window while the user is actively using speech-to-text or video-meeting apps on the mic.
finding: Across three autodoro sessions the user repeatedly hand-curated the mic-exclusion list as new audio apps came online (whisper-writer, Voquill, mint audio control, Google Meet in Chrome). Each time a new speech-to-text or meeting client was introduced, autodoro misfired during real mic use until the user noticed, reported it ('window popped up while in a meeting'), and walked Claude through adding another exemption by name. A durable artifact — for example a learn-mode where autodoro watches pactl source-outputs over N days and auto-suggests additions, or a generic 'any active source-output for >X seconds = active mic session' rule — would replace the per-app manual curation. Three question test: (1) goal = 'don't interrupt me during voice/meetings', (2) recurs for each new audio app the user installs, (3) one config/learning mechanism replaces all future hand-adds. Task, not interaction.
existing_mitigation: Commit 186d4ea switched from PID matching to source-output application.name substring matching, but the allow-list itself is still hand-edited per app.

intent: Produce a fresh set of tour screenshots of the intender Android app on demand.
finding: User asked Claude to 'do a tour' of the Android app to generate welcome/intention-screen screenshots, then again later when the intention screen was missing from the tour. The first attempt wasted time debugging a corrupt PNG (shell stdout prepended to PNG binary from an ad-hoc capture command in a prior session) before a proper tour-screenshot.sh was written. This is a recurring release-prep task — generate marketing/tour screenshots from a clean install of the app — that a single durable script invocation should handle end-to-end. Script now exists (infra/scripts/tour-screenshot.sh), but it's not wired to a single entry-point ('tour the app, drop pngs in repo'); the user still had to orchestrate reset then launch then per-screen taps then capture then commit by prompting. Severity low because a partial mitigation landed in the same session.
existing_mitigation: infra/scripts/tour-screenshot.sh captures individual screens safely; no wrapper orchestrates a full tour end-to-end yet.

intent: Split a batch of unrelated working-copy changes into separate commits by topic before pushing.
finding: At end of session user asked Claude to 'push the current, split it up into commits, one for any spec changes, one for the setup and MCP instructions, and one for the app itself.' This is a repeating pattern across the user's sessions — they work iteratively, accumulate mixed changes, and want clean topic-scoped commits before pushing. A single-prompt skill or slash command that inspects working-copy changes, proposes a topic split, and creates the commits in order would replace the manual per-session instruction. Three-question test: (1) goal = tidy topic-based commits before push, (2) recurs every push batch when changes are mixed, (3) one skill can do the split reliably. Flagged as low because it was a single explicit instance in this window, but the request shape is generic.
existing_mitigation: None found. caveman:caveman-commit generates compressed messages but does not split diffs by topic.
